This can cause unnecessarily large images to be downloaded. 225 226 In addition, `sizes` affects how `srcset` is generated: 227 228 - Without `sizes`: Next.js generates a limited `srcset` (e.g. 1x, 2x), suitable for fixed-size images. 229 - With `sizes`: Next.js generates a full `srcset` (e.g. 640w, 750w, etc.), optimized for responsive layouts. 230 231 > Learn more about `srcset` and `sizes` on [web.dev](https://web.dev/learn/design/responsive-images/#sizes) and [mdn](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/HTML/Element/img#sizes). 232 233 #### `quality` 234 235 An integer between `1` and `100` that sets the quality of the optimized image. Higher values increase file size and visual fidelity. Lower values reduce file size but may affect sharpness. 236 237 ```jsx 238 // Default quality is 75 239 240 ``` 241 242 If you’ve configured [qualities](#qualities) in `next.config.js`, the value must match one of the allowed entries. 243 244 > **Good to know**: If the original image is already low quality, setting a high quality value will increase the file size without improving appearance. 245 246 #### `style` 247 248 Allows passing CSS styles to the underlying image element. 249 250 ```jsx 251 const imageStyle = { 252 borderRadius: '50%', 253 border: '1px solid #fff', 254 width: '100px', 255 height: 'auto', 256 } 257 258 export default function ProfileImage() { 259 return 260 } 261 ``` 262 263 > **Good to know**: If you’re using the `style` prop to set a custom width, be sure to also set `height: 'auto'` to preserve the image’s aspect ratio. 264 265 #### `preload` 266 267 A boolean that indicates if the image should be preloaded. 268 269 ```jsx 270 // Default preload is false 271 272 ``` 273 274 - `true`: [Preloads](https://web.dev/preload-responsive-images/) the image by inserting a `` in the ``. 275 - `false`: Does not preload the image. 276 277 **When to use it:** 278 279 - The image is the [Largest Contentful Paint (LCP)](https://nextjs.org/learn/seo/web-performance/lcp) element. 280 - The image is above the fold, typically the hero image. 281 - You want to begin loading the image in the ``, before its discovered later in the ``. 282 283 **When not to use it:** 284 285 - When you have multiple images that could be considered the [Largest Contentful Paint (LCP)](https://nextjs.org/learn/seo/web-performance/lcp) element depending on the viewport. 286 - When the `loading` property is used. 287 - When the `fetchPriority` property is used. 288 289 In most cases, you should use `loading="eager"` or `fetchPriority="high"` instead of `preload`. 290 291 #### `priority` 292 293 Starting with Next.js 16, the `priority` property has been deprecated in favor of the [`preload`](#preload) property in order to make the behavior clear. 294 295 #### `loading` 296 297 Controls when the image should start loading. 298 299 ```jsx 300 // Defaults to lazy 301 302 ``` 303 304 - `lazy`: Defer loading the image until it reaches a calculated distance from the viewport. 305 - `eager`: Load the image immediately, regardless of its position in the page. 306 307 Use `eager` only when you want to ensure the image is loaded immediately. 308 309 > Learn more about the [`loading` attribute](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/HTML/Element/img#loading). 310 311 #### `placeholder` 312 313 Specifies a placeholder to use while the image is loading, improving the perceived loading performance. 314 315 ```jsx 316 // defaults to empty 317 318 ``` 319 320 - `empty`: No placeholder while the image is loading. 321 - `blur`: Use a blurred version of the image as a placeholder. Must be used with the [`blurDataURL`](#blurdataurl) property. 322 - `data:image/...`: Uses the [Data URL](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/HTTP/Basics_of_HTTP/Data_URIs) as the placeholder. 323 324 **Examples:** 325 326 - [`blur` placeholder](https://image-component.nextjs.gallery/placeholder) 327 - [Shimmer effect with data URL `placeholder` prop](https://image-component.nextjs.gallery/shimmer) 328 - [Color effect with `blurDataURL` prop](https://image-component.nextjs.gallery/color) 329 330 > Learn more about the [`placeholder` attribute](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/HTML/Element/img#placeholder). 331 332 #### `blurDataURL` 333 334 A [Data URL](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/HTTP/Basics_of_HTTP/Data_URIs) to 335 be used as a placeholder image before the image successfully loads. Can be automatically set or used with the [`placeholder="blur"`](#placeholder) property. 336 337 ```jsx 338 339 ``` 340 341 The image is automatically enlarged and blurred, so a very small image (10px or less) is recommended. 342 343 **Automatic** 344 345 If `src` is a static import of a `jpg`, `png`, `webp`, or `avif` file, `blurDataURL` is added automatically—unless the image is animated. 346 347 **Manually set** 348 349 If the image is dynamic or remote, you must provide `blurDataURL` yourself. To generate one, you can use: 350 351 - [A online tool like png-pixel.com](https://png-pixel.com) 352 - [A library like Plaiceholder](https://github.com/joe-bell/plaiceholder) 353 354 A large blurDataURL may hurt performance. Keep it small and simple. 355 356 **Examples:** 357 358 - [Default `blurDataURL` prop](https://image-component.nextjs.gallery/placeholder) 359 - [Color effect with `blurDataURL` prop](https://image-component.nextjs.gallery/color) 360 361 #### `onLoad` 362 363 A callback function that is invoked once the image is completely loaded and the [placeholder](#placeholder) has been removed. 364 365 ```jsx 366 console.log(e.target.naturalWidth)} /> 367 ``` 368 369 The callback function will be called with one argument, the event which has a `target` that references the underlying `` element. 370 371 372 373 > **Good to know**: Using props like `onLoad`, which accept a function, requires using [Client Components](https://react.dev/reference/rsc/use-client) to serialize the provided function. 374 375 376 377 #### `onError` 378 379 A callback function that is invoked if the image fails to load. 380 381 ```jsx 382 console.error(e.target.id)} /> 383 ``` 384 385 386 387 > **Good to know**: Using props like `onError`, which accept a function, requires using [Client Components](https://react.dev/reference/rsc/use-client) to serialize the provided function. 388 389 390 391 #### `unoptimized` 392 393 A boolean that indicates if the image should be optimized. This is useful for images that do not benefit from optimization such as small images (less than 1KB), vector images (SVG), or animated images (GIF). 394 395 ```js 396 import Image from 'next/image' 397 398 const UnoptimizedImage = (props) => { 399 // Default is false 400 return 401 } 402 ``` 403 404 - `true`: The source image will be served as-is from the `src` instead of changing quality, size, or format. 405 - `false`: The source image will be optimized. 406 407 Since Next.js 12.3.0, this prop can be assigned to all images by updating `next.config.js` with the following configuration: 408 409 ```js filename="next.config.js" 410 module.exports = { 411 images: { 412 unoptimized: true, 413 }, 414 } 415 ``` 416 417 #### `overrideSrc` 418 419 When providing the `src` prop to the `` component, both the `srcset` and `src` attributes are generated automatically for the resulting ``.

9	
10	```js filename="next.config.js"
11	module.exports = {
12	  images: {

14	    loaderFile: './my/image/loader.js',
15	  },
16	}
17	```
18	
19	This `loaderFile` must point to a file relative to the root of your Next.js application. The file must export a default function that returns a string, for example:
20

22	
23	```js filename="my/image/loader.js"
24	'use client'
25	
26	export default function myImageLoader({ src, width, quality }) {
27	  return `https://example.com/${src}?w=${width}&q=${quality || 75}`
28	}
29	```
30	
31	Alternatively, you can use the [`loader` prop](/docs/app/api-reference/components/image#loader) to pass the function to each instance of `next/image`.
32	
33	> **Good to know**: Customizing the image loader file, which accepts a function, requires using [Client Components](/docs/app/getting-started/server-and-client-components) to serialize the provided function.

---
title: images
description: Custom configuration for the next/image loader
---

{/* The content of this doc is shared between the app and pages router. You can use the `<PagesOnly>Content</PagesOnly>` component to add content that is specific to the Pages Router. Any shared content should not be wrapped in a component. */}

If you want to use a cloud provider to optimize images instead of using the Next.js built-in Image Optimization API, you can configure `next.config.js` with the following:

```js filename="next.config.js"
module.exports = {
  images: {
    loader: 'custom',
    loaderFile: './my/image/loader.js',
  },
}
```

This `loaderFile` must point to a file relative to the root of your Next.js application. The file must export a default function that returns a string, for example:

<AppOnly>

```js filename="my/image/loader.js"
'use client'

export default function myImageLoader({ src, width, quality }) {
  return `https://example.com/${src}?w=${width}&q=${quality || 75}`
}
```

Alternatively, you can use the [`loader` prop](/docs/app/api-reference/components/image#loader) to pass the function to each instance of `next/image`.

> **Good to know**: Customizing the image loader file, which accepts a function, requires using [Client Components](/docs/app/getting-started/server-and-client-components) to serialize the provided function.

To learn more about configuring the behavior of the built-in [Image Optimization API](/docs/app/api-reference/components/image) and the [Image Component](/docs/app/api-reference/components/image), see [Image Configuration Options](/docs/app/api-reference/components/image#configuration-options) for available options.

</AppOnly>

<PagesOnly>

```js filename="my/image/loader.js"
export default function myImageLoader({ src, width, quality }) {
  return `https://example.com/${src}?w=${width}&q=${quality || 75}`
}
```

Alternatively, you can use the [`loader` prop](/docs/pages/api-reference/components/image#loader) to pass the function to each instance of `next/image`.

To learn more about configuring the behavior of the built-in [Image Optimization API](/docs/pages/api-reference/components/image) and the [Image Component](/docs/pages/api-reference/components/image), see [Image Configuration Options](/docs/pages/api-reference/components/image#configuration-options) for available options.

</PagesOnly>

## Example Loader Configuration

- [Akamai](#akamai)
- [AWS CloudFront](#aws-cloudfront)
- [Cloudinary](#cloudinary)
- [Cloudflare](#cloudflare)
- [Contentful](#contentful)
- [Fastly](#fastly)
- [Gumlet](#gumlet)
- [ImageEngine](#imageengine)
- [Imgix](#imgix)
- [PixelBin](#pixelbin)
- [Sanity](#sanity)
- [Sirv](#sirv)
- [Supabase](#supabase)
- [Thumbor](#thumbor)
- [Imagekit](#imagekitio)
- [Nitrogen AIO](#nitrogen-aio)

### Akamai

```js
// Docs: https://techdocs.akamai.com/ivm/reference/test-images-on-demand
export default function akamaiLoader({ src, width, quality }) {
  return `https://example.com/${src}?imwidth=${width}`
}
```

### AWS CloudFront

```js
// Docs: https://aws.amazon.com/developer/application-security-performance/articles/image-optimization
export default function cloudfrontLoader({ src, width, quality }) {
  const url = new URL(`https://example.com${src}`)
  url.searchParams.set('format', 'auto')
  url.searchParams.set('width', width.toString())
  url.searchParams.set('quality', (quality || 75).toString())
  return url.href
}
```

### Cloudinary

```js
// Demo: https://res.cloudinary.com/demo/image/upload/w_300,c_limit,q_auto/turtles.jpg
export default function cloudinaryLoader({ src, width, quality }) {
  const params = ['f_auto', 'c_limit', `w_${width}`, `q_${quality || 'auto'}`]
  return `https://example.com/${params.join(',')}${src}`
}
```

### Cloudflare

```js
// Docs: https://developers.cloudflare.com/images/transform-images
export default function cloudflareLoader({ src, width, quality }) {
  const params = [`width=${width}`, `quality=${quality || 75}`, 'format=auto']
  return `https://example.com/cdn-cgi/image/${params.join(',')}/${src}`
}
```

### Contentful

```js
// Docs: https://www.contentful.com/developers/docs/references/images-api/
export default function contentfulLoader({ src, width, quality }) {
  const url = new URL(`https://example.com${src}`)
  url.searchParams.set('fm', 'webp')

533:#### `remotePatterns`
534-
535:Use `remotePatterns` in your `next.config.js` file to allow images from specific external paths and block all others. This ensures that only external images from your account can be served.
536-
537-```js filename="next.config.js"
538-module.exports = {
539-  images: {
540:    remotePatterns: [new URL('https://example.com/account123/**')],
541-  },
542-}
543-```
544-
545:You can also configure `remotePatterns` using the object:
546-
547-```js filename="next.config.js"
548-module.exports = {
549-  images: {
550:    remotePatterns: [
551-      {
552-        protocol: 'https',
553-        hostname: 'example.com',
554-        port: '',
555-        pathname: '/account123/**',
556-        search: '',
557-      },
558-    ],
559-  },
560-}
561-```
562-
563-The example above will ensure the `src` property of `next/image` must start with `https://example.com/account123/` and must not have a query string. Any other protocol, hostname, port, or unmatched path will respond with `400` Bad Request.
564-
565-**Wildcard Patterns:**
566-
567-Wildcard patterns can be used for both `pathname` and `hostname` and have the following syntax:
568-
569-- `*` match a single path segment or subdomain
570-- `**` match any number of path segments at the end or subdomains at the beginning. This syntax does not work in the middle of the pattern.
571-
572-```js filename="next.config.js"
573-module.exports = {
574-  images: {
575:    remotePatterns: [
576-      {
577-        protocol: 'https',
578-        hostname: '**.example.com',
579-        port: '',
580-        search: '',
581-      },
582-    ],
583-  },
584-}
585-```
586-
587-This allows subdomains like `image.example.com`. Query strings and custom ports are still blocked.
588-
589-> **Good to know**: When omitting `protocol`, `port`, `pathname`, or `search` then the wildcard `**` is implied. This is not recommended because it may allow malicious actors to optimize urls you did not intend.
590-
591-**Query Strings**:
592-
593-You can also restrict query strings using the `search` property:
594-
595-```js filename="next.config.js"
596-module.exports = {
597-  images: {
598:    remotePatterns: [
599-      {
600-        protocol: 'https',
601-        hostname: 'assets.example.com',
602-        search: '?v=1727111025337',
603-      },
604-    ],
605-  },
606-}
607-```
608-
609-The example above will ensure the `src` property of `next/image` must start with `https://assets.example.com` and must have the exact query string `?v=1727111025337`. Any other protocol or query string will respond with `400` Bad Request.
610-
611:Note that any allowed `remotePatterns` that respond with a redirect will follow the redirect from the remote image server without validating `remotePatterns` again on the redirect location. You can reduce or disable redirects by configuring [maximumRedirects](#maximumredirects).
612-
613-#### `loaderFile`
614-
615-`loaderFiles` allows you to use a custom image optimization service instead of Next.js.
616-
617-```js filename="next.config.js"
618-module.exports = {
619-  images: {
620-    loader: 'custom',

90-#### `alt`
91-
92-The `alt` property is used to describe the image for screen readers and search engines. It is also the fallback text if images have been disabled or an error occurs while loading the image.
93-
94-It should contain text that could replace the image [without changing the meaning of the page](https://html.spec.whatwg.org/multipage/images.html#general-guidelines). It is not meant to supplement the image and should not repeat information that is already provided in the captions above or below the image.

97-
98-> Learn more about [image accessibility guidelines](https://html.spec.whatwg.org/multipage/images.html#alt).

title: "Next.js App Router 入門 — Cache Components で作るミニブログ"
description: |
  Next.js が初めての方向けの Get Started コース。実際にミニブログを作りながら、Next.js 16 の App Router と Cache Components を中心に主要機能を一通り学びます。

  対象: Next.js 未経験。React の関数コンポーネントと hooks、TypeScript の型注釈が読める方を想定しています。HTML/CSS の基本知識とターミナル操作も前提とします。

  扱う内容: ファイルベースルーティング、Server / Client Components、Server Actions、Cache Components（'use cache' / cacheLife / cacheTag / updateTag）、Error / Loading UI、Metadata、Vercel デプロイ。

+  扱う内容: ファイルベースルーティング、Server / Client Components、Server Actions、Cache Components（'use cache' / cacheLife / cacheTag / updateTag）、Error / Loading UI、Metadata、画像最適化（next/image）、Vercel デプロイ。
slug: "nextjs-app-router-getting-started"
tags:

content.text:
完了しました。

**変更内容**
- 新規トピック `06-04-image-optimization.md` 作成（バンドル docs ベース）
  - 知識: `<Image>` の 4 機能、ローカル/リモート、`width`/`height`/`fill`、`sizes`、lazy/`preload`、`placeholder`/`blurDataURL`、`alt`、`unoptimized`
  - **重要**: Next.js 16 で `priority` が **非推奨**となり `preload` に置換された旨を明記（`node_modules/next/dist/docs/01-app/03-api-reference/02-components/image.md` line 291-293 に基づく）
  - ステップ: data に cover フィールド追加 → `images.remotePatterns` 設定 → 一覧サムネイル → 詳細ヒーロー（`preload`） → 動作確認
- `course.yaml`: ch6 の topics に追加、ch6 description と扱う内容に「画像最適化」を追記

参照したバンドル docs:
- `01-app/01-getting-started/12-images.md`
- `01-app/03-api-reference/02-components/image.md`
- `01-app/03-api-reference/05-config/01-next-config-js/images.md`

re-seed が必要なら指示してください。

message.content: ここって public で置いたファイルを URL で取得するのと import して src に渡すのが混じってない？ import するのは別に public に置く必要ないし、 public に置いたファイルはローカル画像と表現するのはちょっと違う気がするけど

attachment.content:
```tsx
import Image from "next/image";

<Image src="/cover.png" alt="表紙" width={1200} height={600} />;
```

### ローカル画像

`public/` ディレクトリに置いたファイルは絶対パス（`/cover.png` など）で参照できます。さらに静的 import すると、`width` / `height` / `blurDataURL` が自動で埋まります。

```tsx
import Image from "next/image";
import cover from "./cover.png"; // jpg/png/webp/avif の静的 import

<Image src={cover} alt="表紙" />; // width/height/blurDataURL 不要
```

content.text:
おっしゃる通りです。2 つを混同していました:
- `public/` 配下 + URL 文字列参照 → ファイルは bundler を通らないので `width`/`height` 自動取得は **無い**
- 静的 import → 画像ファイルは bundler を通るので自動取得が **ある**。ファイルの置き場所は `public/` でなくてよい

「ローカル画像」というラベルも、場所と機構が違う 2 つを束ねてしまっています。書き直します。

input.old_string:
### ローカル画像

`public/` ディレクトリに置いたファイルは絶対パス（`/cover.png` など）で参照できます。さらに静的 import すると、`width` / `height` / `blurDataURL` が自動で埋まります。

```tsx
import Image from "next/image";
import cover from "./cover.png"; // jpg/png/webp/avif の静的 import

<Image src={cover} alt="表紙" />; // width/height/blurDataURL 不要
```
input.new_string:
### プロジェクト内の画像

プロジェクトに同梱した画像を `<Image>` に渡す方法は、別の仕組みである 2 通りに分かれます。

**1. `public/` 配下のファイルを URL 文字列で参照する**

`public/` ディレクトリに置いたファイルはルート URL（`/cover.png` など）で配信されます。`<Image>` には文字列パスを渡します。この経路は **ファイルがバンドラを通らない** ため、`width` と `height` を自前で指定する必要があります（`blurDataURL` も自動では埋まりません）。

```tsx
import Image from "next/image";

<Image src="/cover.png" alt="表紙" width={1200} height={600} />;
```

**2. 画像ファイルを静的 import する**

jpg / png / webp / avif ファイルを **ソースコード内のどこからでも**（`public/` に置く必要はなく、コンポーネントの隣でも `app/` 配下でもよい）`import cover from './cover.png'` の形で import できます。こちらは画像ファイルがバンドラを通るため、Next.js がビルド時に画像を解析し、**`width` ・ `height` ・ `blurDataURL` が自動で埋まります**。

```tsx
import Image from "next/image";
import cover from "./cover.png"; // 例: コンポーネントの隣に置いた画像

<Image src={cover} alt="表紙" />; // width/height/blurDataURL 不要
```

`public/` の URL 参照は「アプリのアセットとして単に配信する」、静的 import は「コードと一緒にバンドラに食わせて最適化情報を取り出す」という別の仕組みなので、両者を混同しないようにしてください。

-`public/` ディレクトリに置いたファイルは絶対パス（`/cover.png` など）で参照できます。さらに静的 import すると、`width` / `height` / `blurDataURL` が自動で埋まります。
+プロジェクトに同梱した画像を `<Image>` に渡す方法は、別の仕組みである 2 通りに分かれます。

```tsx

-import cover from "./cover.png"; // jpg/png/webp/avif の静的 import
+<Image src="/cover.png" alt="表紙" width={1200} height={600} />;
+**2. 画像ファイルを静的 import する**

+jpg / png / webp / avif ファイルを **ソースコード内のどこからでも**（`public/` に置く必要はなく、コンポーネントの隣でも `app/` 配下でもよい）`import cover from './cover.png'` の形で import できます。こちらは画像ファイルがバンドラを通るため、Next.js がビルド時に画像を解析し、**`width` ・ `height` ・ `blurDataURL` が自動で埋まります**。
+```tsx
+import Image from "next/image";
+import cover from "./cover.png"; // 例: コンポーネントの隣に置いた画像
<Image src={cover} alt="表紙" />; // width/height/blurDataURL 不要
+`public/` の URL 参照は「アプリのアセットとして単に配信する」、静的 import は「コードと一緒にバンドラに食わせて最適化情報を取り出す」という別の仕組みなので、両者を混同しないようにしてください。
### リモート画像

外部 URL の画像を扱うには、**`next.config.ts` の `images.remotePatterns` で明示的にホストを許可** する必要があります（許可していないホストへのリクエストは `400 Bad Request` で拒否される）。リモート画像はビルド時に取得できないため、`width` / `height` を必ず手動で指定するか、`fill` で親要素に追従させます。

content.text:
修正しました。

- ラベルを「ローカル画像」→「プロジェクト内の画像」に変更
- 2 通りを **別の仕組み**として明確に分割:
  1. `public/` + URL 文字列: バンドラを通らないので `width`/`height` 自前指定、`blurDataURL` も無し
  2. 静的 import: 場所は `public/` でなくてよい（コンポーネント隣でも `app/` 内でもよい）。バンドラが解析して `width`/`height`/`blurDataURL` を自動付与
- 末尾に「両者を混同しない」と注意書き
